claims: TDD starts at agentic_eval_runner public boundary
acceleration, qualification, latency, and replay-hash tests planned
acceleration ratios and quality-gated predicates tested
agentic eval bridge and corpus tests remain green
receipts.command: uv run pytest tests/test_agentic_eval.py tests/test_agentic_eval_bridge.py tests/test_agentic_eval_corpus.py -q
uv run python -m py_compile supervisor/agentic_eval.py supervisor/agentic_eval_assembler.py && git diff --check

claims: agentic_allowed and agentic_required qualify at equal budget

assumptions_to_verify: bb6c7ba4 SHA is stale documentation only, not a regenerated divergent corpus
fixture golden hash 5f5239f0 remains the CI-stable path separate from parallelism artifact
contradictions_checked: git diff --stat 633 insertions/3 deletions across 4 files: MATCH
state.py in diff: EMPTY
_acceleration_ratio/_qualification_result/_build_report_only_recommendation at claimed lines: PRESENT
report-only checks vs parallelism report + test-evidence: MATCH
acceleration p50/p95/qualifies table vs report.json summary: MATCH
test-evidence SHA bb6c7ba4 vs shasum report.json 628f7d73: MISMATCH (documented)
Claude 17/33/678 claims vs independent pytest: MATCH (678 passed in 131.73s)
missing_evidence: corrected SHA pin in test-evidence for current report.json
handoff planning-artifact SHA re-hash (not re-verified this gate)
critical_review.strongest_objection: test-evidence.md pins parallelism report SHA bb6c7ba4… but on-disk report.json hashes to 628f7d73… (P4 hash pin drift; content/metrics still match).

tests: uv run pytest tests/test_agentic_eval.py -q → 17 passed
uv run pytest tests/test_agentic_eval.py tests/test_agentic_eval_bridge.py tests/test_agentic_eval_corpus.py -q → 33 passed
uv run --extra dev pytest -q → 678 passed

assumptions_to_verify: Self-reported pytest results reflect the current frozen diff.
Untracked docs and scratch files are intended workflow artifacts and not hidden implementation scope.
The report-only recommended_policy value operator_review_required is acceptable because policy_mutated remains false and default_change_allowed remains false.
contradictions_checked: git diff --stat matches the claimed 4 files/633 insertions/3 deletions.
Tracked changed files match the claimed implementation/test changed_files list.
git diff -- supervisor/state.py produced no tracked diff.
test-evidence Report SHA256 matches report.json .report_sha256; raw file checksum differs, but that is not the asserted content hash.
report.json and comparison-summary.json keep default_change_allowed false, recommendation.report_only true, recommendation.policy_mutated false, and agentic_lead_policy off.
agentic_allowed and agentic_required qualify at equal budget in the report; lead_direct is not recommended as a fan-out mode.
missing_evidence: Fresh independent pytest execution against the current worktree.
Canonical handoff hashes for all planning/outcome artifacts; I only checked the report artifact's internal SHA and current file presence.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The gate did not independently rerun pytest; acceptance relies on self-reported test-evidence plus static inspection of the current diff and artifacts.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Any independent test failure, state/config mutation, report_only false, default_change_allowed true, policy_mutated true, a blocked or lower-quality arm qualifying, or a canonical artifact hash mismatch would change this decision.

uv run python -m py_compile supervisor/agentic_eval.py supervisor/agentic_eval_assembler.py && git diff --check (self-reported: passed)
